steps.title: Test Equation I
steps.content: Multiply the given equation $4x + 7y = 8$ by 1.5: $6x + 10.5y = 12$. This is identical to Equation I, so both represent the same line and have infinitely many solutions. Equation I works.

steps.title: Test Equation II
steps.content: For Equation II ($6x - 10.5y = 12$) to be the same line, we'd need a scalar $k$ where $4k=6$ and $7k=-10.5$. The first gives $k=1.5$, the second gives $k=-1.5$, which is a contradiction. So the lines are not parallel; they intersect at exactly one point. Equation II also works.

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content:
Both Equation I and Equation II can be the second equation in a system with at least one solution.

C is correct.
